config: Reframe (naive multi-agent vs pigeon multi-agent), same model (claude/sonnet), 3-agent chain plan->implement->review
purpose: Decisive LARGE-CONTEXT test: does pigeon win in its best case (large files requiring exploration, multi-agent chain)? Designed after cookiecutter showed pigeon net-negative on small tasks.
harness: held-out acceptance test (never shown to agents) + full suite as regression gate; gate validated to fail pristine / pass a reference impl

task.name: t1-slug
task.description: Add a `Slug` validator (validate.py, 708 lines) and a `Slug` String-field that wires it (fields.py, 2148 lines), mirroring the Email/Url/Regexp conventions, exported as marshmallow.validate.Slug and marshmallow.fields.Slug.
task.why_pigeons_best_case: Both files are large, so agents must explore to follow conventions -> the regime where pigeon's pack/retrieve curated context could cut exploration turns, and where its fixed overhead is a small fraction of a heavy task.

agents: claude/sonnet x3 (plan, implement, review), hand-rolled, filesystem-shared, plan text forwarded as the manual handoff (NO source-file pasting -> honest realistic baseline)
claude/sonnet x3 via pigeon coordinate (plan->implement->review), pack + retrieve + handoff pointers

pigeon_internal_accounting.note: pigeon's pointers-vs-payloads metric vs a re-transmission baseline; NOT a real total-cost saving

delta_pigeon_vs_naive.est_cost_usd: +8.1% (pigeon still more expensive, but near parity)
delta_pigeon_vs_naive.token_basis_caveat: Headline metric is COST (both from claude total_cost_usd). naive tokens = claude usage incl cache; pigeon tokens = pigeon tiktoken accounting -> different bases, not comparable.

task.verdict: DECISIVE: pigeon does NOT win on cost even in its best case. The penalty amortized from +59% (small) to +8% (large) -- overhead is ~fixed and shrinks with task size -- but never crossed into savings, and pigeon was ~80% slower in this sequential chain. pigeon's pack/retrieve did not cut the exploration cost (plan step ~parity). Conclusion: pigeon's value is NOT token savings.
caveats_and_untested: Sequential only (parallel_limit 1, and a linear plan->implement->review chain). pigeon's coordinate can run INDEPENDENT subtasks in parallel; hand-rolled sequential coordination cannot easily. On workloads with independent parallel subtasks, pigeon could win on WALL-CLOCK -- this regime was not tested.
Single model (sonnet) on both arms. pigeon's cross-CLI value (handing off between claude/opencode/gemini, which share no memory) is structural, not captured by a same-model cost benchmark.
Automation/observability/reproducibility (run manifests, per-model token accounting, audit trail) are real pigeon capabilities a cost benchmark does not measure.
